seoTitle: How to Share a Microsoft Forms Presentation Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to use Microsoft Forms' Present Mode to display live survey or quiz results with QR codes and PowerPoint integration.
intro: Running a live poll or quiz during a webinar or training session works best when responses show up on screen in real time without switching windows. This guide covers activating Forms' Present Mode to generate an instant QR code for attendees, embedding a form directly inside a PowerPoint slide, and switching between chart views as responses come in. It's built for presenters, trainers, and educators running interactive sessions.

steps.heading: Open your form
steps.body: Select your target survey or quiz from your Microsoft Forms dashboard.

steps.heading: Activate Present Mode
steps.body: Click the Present icon in the top-right toolbar to expand the layout into full-screen interactive mode with an auto-generated QR code.

steps.heading: Let attendees join
steps.body: Instruct your audience to scan the on-screen QR code with their phones to open the questionnaire instantly.

steps.heading: Watch live results
steps.body: Watch incoming responses stream in as interactive charts update dynamically, and use the layout toggle to switch chart types.

steps.heading: Embed in PowerPoint
steps.body: In PowerPoint, click Insert, choose the Forms add-in, paste your form's web address, select the interactive meeting preset, and launch presentation mode.

faq.question: Do attendees need a Microsoft account to respond?
faq.answer: It depends on your form's permission settings; you can configure it to allow anyone to respond or restrict it to your organization's accounts.

faq.question: Can I embed a live poll directly inside my PowerPoint slides?
faq.answer: Yes, the native Forms add-in in PowerPoint lets you paste your form's link and present it as an interactive slide.

faq.question: How do attendees join the live form quickly?
faq.answer: Present Mode generates an on-screen QR code and short URL that attendees can scan or type to join instantly from their phones.

faq.question: Can I switch between chart types while presenting?
faq.answer: Yes, use the layout toggle at the bottom of the presentation screen to swap between different live result visualizations.
